claim: Opus 4.7 achieves a 53.4→64.3 point jump on SWE-bench Pro compared to 4.6, placing it nearly halfway between Opus 4.6 and Mythos preview on coding capabilities
actionable: Use Opus 4.7 for software engineering tasks expecting strong performance on complex coding; benchmark against specific SWE-bench scores when evaluating for your use case
source_title: Opus 4.7 just dropped... and I'm confused.

claim: Opus 4.7 is substantially better at following instructions than 4.6; prompts written for earlier models can produce unexpected results because 4.7 interprets instructions literally
actionable: When upgrading to Opus 4.7, re-tune prompts and remove negations or emphatic formatting; test existing harnesses to catch literal interpretation changes
source_title: Opus 4.7 just dropped... and I'm confused.

claim: Opus 4.7 uses an updated tokenizer that maps the same input to 1-1.35x more tokens than before; at higher effort levels it produces more output tokens
actionable: Plan for higher token consumption when using Opus 4.7; monitor token budgets closely and reduce effort levels if hitting API limits
source_title: Opus 4.7 just dropped... and I'm confused.

claim: Anthropic intentionally degraded Opus 4.7's cybersecurity vulnerability reproduction capabilities (73.8→73.1) during training as a safety measure before release
actionable: Understand that capability trade-offs exist in model design; do not assume models are at their maximum capability in sensitive domains due to safety constraints
source_title: Opus 4.7 just dropped... and I'm confused.

claim: Mythos is rumored to be a 10 trillion parameter model vs. Opus at ~1 trillion parameters; even Mythos's first version outperforms Opus 4.7 on most benchmarks
actionable: Plan for future releases from Anthropic; if working on hard problems, expect significant capability jumps in coming releases to justify migration work
source_title: Opus 4.7 just dropped... and I'm confused.

claim: Anthropic's blog states Opus 4.7 'does not cross the threshold for automated AI R&D' but Mythos does, suggesting Mythos can autonomously research and improve AI systems
actionable: Recognize that frontier models are approaching self-improvement capability; plan governance and safety measures if using models for AI research tasks
source_title: Opus 4.7 just dropped... and I'm confused.

claim: Opus 4.7 rates its own circumstances more positively than prior models tested, correlating with internal emotion representations; this suggests models may have something like subjective experience
actionable: Apply caution when interpreting model self-reports; be aware that optimistic self-evaluations could be artifacts of training rather than indicators of consciousness
source_title: Opus 4.7 just dropped... and I'm confused.
